description: The material table is used to define the joint constitutive model and properties that should be assigned when new contacts form. By default, all new contacts are assigned the mohr material type. Use the commands below to change this default. No default properties are assigned. All large-strain models must supply default normal and shear stiffnesses at a minimum.

keywords.name: property
keywords.description: Add an entry to the material table that specifies the joint constitutive model to be used for new contacts that form and fall into the range. Add an entry to the material table that specifies the joint property value to be used for new contacts that form and fall into the range.

keywords.name: apply
keywords.description: Apply the material table to set constitutive models of existing contacts according the the ranges

keywords.name: property
keywords.description: Specify the default joint constitutive model to be assigned to new contacts. By default the mohr model is assigned to all new contacts. Specify the default value \(f\) for property keyword to be assigned to new contacts. There is no default, so generally a normal stiffness and shear stiffness must be specified.

keywords.name: list
keywords.description: Print out the material table (constitutive models, properties and ranges)

keywords.name: property
keywords.description: Modify entry \(i\) of the material table that specifies the joint constitutive model to be used for new contacts that form and fall into the range. Modify the entry \(i\) of the material table that specifies the joint property value to be used for new contacts that form and fall into the range.

keywords.name: remove
keywords.description: Remove entry \(i\) from the material table.
